name: Moghul Style Mixed Vegetables with Paneer
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup mixed nuts (cashews and pistachios are good)
1/2 cup green bell pepper, cut into 1-inch diamonds
1/2 cup red bell pepper, cut into 1-inch diamonds
1 medium carrot, sliced and then cut into diamonds
1/4 cup green beans, cut into 1-inch diagonal pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 3 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add nuts and stir-fry until browned. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
3. Add raisins and fry until plump. Remove and set aside.
4. Add the remaining butter to the skillet.
5. Add garlic and onion, and cook until lightly browned, about 4 minutes.
6. Stir in tomato and cook until soft.
7. Add the remaining vegetables and stir-fry for 6 minutes.
8. Stir in spices, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and cook until vegetables are tender, 8 to 10 minutes.
9. Stir in paneer, cream, and pineapple. Cook until heated through.
10. Transfer to a serving dish and garnish with fried nuts and raisins.
PER SERVING: 350 calories, 13 g protein, 20 g carbohydrate, 26 g fat (15 g saturated), cholesterol not available, 424 mg sodium, 3 g fiber.
